nonGoals: Do NOT modify engine, report, or test code
Do NOT change existing GradeF or StatusFail behavior
Do NOT wire up callers — constants only
objective: Add GradeNA and StatusUnsupported constants to the domain model as the foundation for unsupported-language handling
planText: Add GradeNA constant to internal/domain/dimension.go — a new grade value that maps to the string "N/A". Update GradeFromScore (or add a companion function) so that score 0 for an unsupported language returns GradeNA rather than GradeF. Add StatusUnsupported constant to internal/domain/record.go with the string value "unsupported". Ensure IsPassing() returns false for StatusUnsupported. These are pure additive changes — no existing behavior is altered, and no callers are wired up yet.

nonGoals: Do NOT modify any test files
Do NOT add any new functionality
Do NOT touch the analysis registry or ForLanguage function
objective: Strip all Swift-specific production code and delete the swift-standard policy pack
planText: In cmd/certify/init_cmd.go: delete the swiftStandardPolicy constant (lines ~704-738, the multi-line YAML string literal) and delete the `case "swift": return swiftStandardPolicy` branch inside languagePolicy() (line ~306-307). Delete the file testdata/policies/swift-standard.yml entirely. These removals reflect that Swift has no structural analyzer registered and is no longer a supported language. After this change the project still compiles but existing tests referencing Swift will fail — those are fixed in the next deliverable.

nonGoals: Do NOT modify production code
Do NOT add new test files
Do NOT add N/A-grade or unsupported-banner test expectations (that comes later)
objective: Fix existing test expectations that still reference Swift after its removal
planText: In cmd/certify/cli_test.go: remove "swift" from the language slice in TestLanguagePolicy_AllSupported (line ~93) — change from `["go", "typescript", "javascript", "python", "rust", "swift"]` to `["go", "typescript", "javascript", "python", "rust"]`. In internal/config/policy_test.go: update TestLoadPolicyPacks_Dir (line ~89) — change the expected pack count assertion from 7 to 6. Update the expected-names loop (line ~99) — remove "swift-standard" from the expected list so it becomes `["go-standard", "go-library", "python-standard", "rust-standard", "security", "ts-standard"]`. These are the Category A test failures identified in the investigation.

nonGoals: Do NOT modify report layer (card.go, full.go)
Do NOT create test files
Do NOT change policy loading or the analysis registry
objective: Ensure the certifier emits N/A grade and Unsupported status for units in languages with no registered analyzer
planText: In internal/engine/certifier.go (or pipeline.go — whichever contains CertifyUnit): at the top of the certification path for a unit, call ForLanguage(unit.Language). If it returns nil (no analyzer registered), short-circuit and return a Record with StatusUnsupported, GradeNA, and score 0 — do not proceed through the normal scoring/evidence pipeline. This ensures Swift units (and any future unregistered language) are explicitly marked as unsupported rather than silently receiving a misleading F grade through score-0→GradeF fallthrough.

nonGoals: Do NOT modify domain or engine code
Do NOT add test cases to report tests
Do NOT change the card's JSON serialization format other than the new field
objective: Add unsupported-language banner to report card and exclude unsupported records from aggregate scoring in full report
planText: In internal/report/card.go: add an UnsupportedLanguages field to the Card struct. In GenerateCard, detect records with StatusUnsupported and populate this field. In the text/markdown formatter, render an unsupported banner (e.g. "⚠ Unsupported languages detected: …") when UnsupportedLanguages is non-empty. Exclude unsupported records from the overall score calculation. In internal/report/full.go: in buildLanguageDetail, skip records with StatusUnsupported when computing per-language aggregate scores and grade distributions. Unsupported-language units should appear in the detail listing (so users see them) but must not drag down aggregate grades.

nonGoals: Do NOT modify production code
Do NOT modify existing test files
Do NOT add tests for report-layer behavior
objective: Create language_tiers_test.go to lock in the analyzer-registry contract
planText: Create internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go with the following test functions: (1) TestForLanguage_SupportedLanguages — verify ForLanguage returns non-nil for "go", "ts", "js", "py", "rs". (2) TestForLanguage_SwiftReturnsNil — verify ForLanguage("swift") returns nil. (3) TestCertifier_UnsupportedLanguageYieldsNA — create a unit with language "swift" and verify the certifier returns a record with StatusUnsupported and GradeNA (not GradeF). Use the existing test helper patterns from the codebase (e.g. table-driven tests). These tests protect against accidental Swift re-introduction or registry misconfiguration.

nonGoals: Do NOT modify production code
Do NOT create new test files
Do NOT modify engine or domain tests
objective: Add N/A-grade, unsupported-banner, and unsupported-exclusion test expectations to report tests
planText: In internal/report/card_test.go: add a test case that includes a record with StatusUnsupported and GradeNA, and verify the generated card (a) renders an unsupported-language banner, (b) shows "N/A" as the grade for that unit, and (c) excludes the unsupported unit from the overall aggregate score. In internal/report/full_test.go: add a test case verifying that buildLanguageDetail (or its equivalent) skips StatusUnsupported records when computing per-language aggregates — unsupported-language units appear in listings but do not affect grade distributions or aggregate scores. Use the existing test patterns and helper functions already in those test files.
